puzzle.title: The Appointed Leader of a Nation
puzzle.intro: In a time of great challenge and despair for a people, a divine appointment was made. This individual, initially overlooked by many, was chosen by Allah to lead a nation against a formidable adversary. Their story is a testament to the power of faith and the wisdom behind divine decree, showing that true leadership is not always found in expected places.
clues: I was chosen by Allah to be a leader for my people when they sought a ruler to fight in His cause.
My people initially questioned my appointment, feeling that others were more deserving due to their wealth or lineage.
A sign of my rightful leadership was the return of a sacred chest, containing relics from previous prophets, which brought tranquility.
I led my army to a river, where I tested their obedience by forbidding them to drink more than a single scoop of water.
Despite the small number of those who remained steadfast after the test, we faced a mighty warrior and their army.
Under my command, a young individual from my army, who was not a renowned fighter, bravely confronted and defeated the formidable enemy champion.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: The appointment as a leader against a giant
The flood that engulfed the land
trial.answer: The appointment as a leader against a giant

outcome.label: Outcome
options: A kingdom was established with immense wealth and power.
The people were drowned in a great flood.
The enemy's champion was defeated, and Allah granted victory.
A great drought afflicted the land for years.
The leader was swallowed by a large creature.
outcome.answer: The enemy's champion was defeated, and Allah granted victory.

puzzle.arabic: فَلَمَّا فَصَلَ طَالُوتُ بِٱلْجُنُودِ قَالَ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُبْتَلِيكُم بِنَهَرٍۢ فَمَن شَرِبَ مِنْهُ فَلَيْسَ مِنِّى وَمَن لَّمْ يَطْعَمْهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ مِنِّىٓ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱغْتَرَفَ غُرْفَةًۢ بِيَدِهِۦ ۚ فَشَرِبُوا۟ مِنْهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلًۭا مِّنْهُمْ ۚ فَلَمَّا جَاوَزَهُۥ هُوَ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ قَالُوا۟ لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا ٱلْيَوْمَ بِجَالُوتَ وَجُنُودِهِۦ ۚ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَـٰقُوا۟ ٱللَّهِ كَم مِّن فِئَةٍۢ قَلِيلَةٍ غَلَبَتْ فِئَةًۭ كَثِيرَةًۢ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ
puzzle.verse: And when Saul went forth with the soldiers, he said, "Indeed, Allāh will be testing you with a river. So whoever drinks from it is not of me, and whoever does not taste it is indeed of me, excepting one who takes [from it] in the hollow of his hand." But they drank from it, except a [very] few of them. Then when he had crossed it along with those who believed with him, they said, "There is no power for us today against Goliath and his soldiers." But those who were certain that they would meet Allāh said, "How many a small company has overcome a large company by permission of Allāh. And Allāh is with the patient." (2:249)
